title: Ghormeh Sabzi
ingredients: Oil, salt, pepper, turmeric as needed
steps: Dice the onions and fry in oil until golden.
Add the meat, turmeric, and pepper. Brown on all sides.
Add the soaked kidney beans and 3 cups of water. Bring to a boil.
In a separate pan, fry the chopped herbs with oil until dark and aromatic.
Add the fried herbs and pierced dried limes to the stew.
Lower the heat and simmer for 2–3 hours until the meat is tender.
Season with salt near the end and serve with steamed rice.

title: California Sushi
ingredients: Soy sauce, pickled ginger, wasabi for serving
steps: Place a nori sheet on a bamboo mat, shiny side down.
Spread an even layer of sushi rice on the nori, leaving a 1cm border.
Sprinkle sesame seeds over the rice.
Flip the nori so the rice faces down on the mat.
Lay avocado, cucumber, and crab sticks along the center.
Roll tightly using the mat, applying gentle pressure.
Slice into 8 pieces with a wet knife. Serve with soy sauce, ginger, and wasabi.

title: Pepperoni Pizza
steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (430°F).
Roll out the pizza dough on a floured surface.
Spread the pizza sauce evenly, leaving a border.
Top with mozzarella cheese and arrange pepperoni slices.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle oregano.
Bake for 12–15 minutes until the crust is golden and cheese bubbles.
Slice and serve hot with chili flakes if desired.

title: Kebab Koobideh
ingredients: Lavash bread and fresh herbs for serving
steps: Mix the ground meat with grated onion, salt, pepper, and turmeric.
Knead the mixture well for 5 minutes until sticky.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Take a handful of meat and press it around flat metal skewers.
Grill over hot charcoal, turning frequently until cooked through.
Serve on lavash bread with sumac, grilled tomatoes, and fresh herbs.

title: Ravioli Pasta
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Cook the ravioli according to package instructions (about 3-4 minutes).
While pasta cooks, melt butter in a pan and add garlic and sage.
Cook until butter turns golden and fragrant. Remove from heat.
Drain the ravioli and add to the butter sauce, tossing gently.
Serve with grated Parmesan and black pepper.

title: Pancakes
ingredients: Maple syrup and fresh fruits for topping
steps: Sift together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt.
In another bowl, whisk milk, egg, and melted butter.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients. Stir until just combined (lumps are okay).
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and lightly grease.
Pour 1/4 cup of batter for each pancake. Cook until bubbles form, then flip.
Cook until golden brown on both sides. Serve with syrup and fruits.

title: Caesar Salad
steps: Wash and dry the romaine lettuce, then chop into bite-sized pieces.
In a large bowl, toss lettuce with Caesar dressing.
Add croutons and half of the Parmesan. Toss again.
Top with sliced grilled chicken if desired.
Garnish with remaining Parmesan and serve with lemon wedges.

title: Strawberry Matcha Iced Latte
steps: Sift matcha powder into a bowl. Add hot water and whisk until smooth.
Drizzle strawberry syrup into a tall glass.
Add ice cubes and pour milk over the ice.
Slowly pour the matcha over the milk for a layered effect.
Stir before drinking. Garnish with fresh strawberry slices.

title: Omelette
ingredients: Fillings: cheese, diced ham, bell peppers, mushrooms (optional)
steps: Whisk eggs, milk, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Heat butter in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Pour in the egg mixture and let it set slightly.
Add your desired fillings on one half of the omelette.
Fold the other half over the fillings. Cook for 1 more minute.
Slide onto a plate and serve immediately.

title: Ash Reshteh
steps: Cook chickpeas, beans, and lentils in plenty of water until tender.
Sauté sliced onions in oil until golden. Set some aside for garnish.
Add the chopped herbs to the pot and simmer for 20 minutes.
Break the noodles and add to the soup. Cook until noodles are soft.
In a small pan, fry dried mint in oil for a few seconds and add to the soup.
Season with salt and turmeric. Serve in bowls with kashk and fried onions.
